questions.question: Which type of keyword usually has the highest conversion rate for a solo founder?

options.text: Head Keywords (e.g., 'Marketing')
Generic Keywords (e.g., 'Business Tips')
Solution-Aware / Comparison Keywords (e.g., 'Apollo vs. Sales Navigator')
Short-Tail Keywords (e.g., 'CRM')

questions.explanation: Solution-aware or comparison keywords target users who are ready to buy or make a decision. They have lower volume than head keywords but much higher conversion intent.

questions.question: What is the correct rule for using H1 and H2 tags on a page?

options.text: Use as many H1s as possible to emphasize importance.
Use H2s for the main title and H1s for sub-topics.
Use exactly one H1 for the main topic and H2s for sub-topics/pillars.
It doesn't matter; search engines ignore header tags.

questions.explanation: A page should have exactly one H1 tag that clearly states the main topic. H2 and H3 tags should be used to create a logical hierarchy of sub-topics, helping both users and AI models parse the content.

questions.question: How does Technical SEO primarily serve as a 'Hygiene Factor' in 2025?

options.text: It keeps your code clean and readable for your engineer.
It ensures your site is fast, secure, and mobile-friendly, which are the 'entrance fees' for search visibility.
It automatically generates content for your blog.
It prevents users from taking screenshots of your site.

questions.explanation: Google and users expect a baseline of performance. If your site is slow, non-secure (no HTTPS), or broken on mobile, you will be penalized in rankings regardless of how good your content is.

questions.question: Which tool is considered the most important 'Free' asset for monitoring your SEO performance?

options.text: Instagram Insights
Google Search Console
Microsoft Word Spellcheck
ChatGPT

questions.explanation: Google Search Console provides direct data from Google about which queries are driving traffic to your site, your average rankings, and any technical errors found by Google's crawlers.

questions.question: If you are a Creator/Coach, what should be the primary focus of your On-Page Optimization?

options.text: Optimizing for broad keywords like 'Life Coach'.
Ensuring your course curriculum and outcome pages are optimized for specific transformations and student pains.
Hiding keywords in white text so search engines find them but users don't.
Removing all text and using only images to look 'premium'.

questions.explanation: For creators, SEO is about demonstrating expertise in a specific niche. Optimizing your curriculum and outcome pages ensures that when a prospect searches for a transformation you provide, you are the visible expert.

questions.question: Pick one 'Giant' in your industry (a large competitor or established brand). What is one 'Long-Tail' or 'Comparison' query where you could provide a better, more targeted answer than their generic documentation or blog?
promptHints: Think of a specific use case the giant ignores.
Consider a 'versus' query where you can highlight your unique approach.
Identify a common frustration users have with the giant's product.
